title: Beta/gamma impossibility under Principle II: formal argument from c002-c007

content.finding:
Formal impossibility argument assembled from six independent experimental constraints (c002-c007). Beta and gamma cannot be made adaptive through any self-generated signal under the current architecture.

The evidence chain:
1. Beta/gamma adaptation requires global information (c007 — structurally global coupling)
2. No local statistic correlates with that global effect (c003, best r=0.44 vs threshold 0.7)
3. Analytical gradient is 100-1000x too weak and directionally unreliable (c002)
4. Per-cell decomposition destroys the computation (c006, 53% MI loss)
5. External MI measurement works but violates Principle II (c004)
6. Even with a working signal, landscape has no unique optimum (c005)

The gap is ARCHITECTURAL: beta/gamma require global information to adapt; the computation produces only local information per cell. No bridge exists between local dynamics and global coupling sensitivity within this architecture.

Four escape conditions documented:
1. New local statistic with r > 0.7 (theoretically unlikely per c007)
2. Architecture modification introducing lateral signaling / second field / hierarchy
3. Different decomposition (single scalar parameterizing beta/gamma family) — NOT FULLY CLOSED
4. Topology adaptation (Stage 5) making beta/gamma structurally irrelevant
5. Relaxing Principle II (constitutional argument, not experimental)
content.implication: Beta/gamma are permanently frozen under the current architecture and Principle II. Stage 4 cannot reduce the frozen frame through beta/gamma. The only path through beta/gamma is an architecture change (Escape Condition 2 or 4) or a constitutional relaxation (Escape Condition 5). This is NOT a failure — it's a precise characterization of where the frozen frame floor lies.
